title: Scale CloudNative-PG PostgreSQL to 3 Replicas with PgBouncer and Automated Backups
subtasks: Update CloudNative-PG Cluster CR spec.instances from 2 to 3 for full HA (1 primary + 2 replicas). Verify streaming replication on both replicas via pg_stat_replication.

Enable PgBouncer connection pooling by adding spec.pgbouncer block to the CNPG Cluster CR: poolMode: transaction, defaultPoolSize: 20, maxClientConnections: 200. Create the PgBouncer-fronted Service endpoint and update sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap CNPG_SIGMA1_URL to route through PgBouncer.

Configure automated daily base backups via CNPG spec.backup.scheduledBackups with schedule '0 2 * * *' targeting the R2 barmanObjectStore (sigma1-wal-archive bucket). Verify WAL archiving is continuous (already enabled in Task 10) and base backups complete successfully.

Create a recovery test CronJob that monthly restores from the latest backup to a temporary CNPG cluster, runs a schema validation query, and deletes the temp cluster. Log results to stdout for Loki pickup.
Validate replication lag stays under 100ms under load by running a pg_stat_replication query during a k6 write workload. Document acceptable lag thresholds in ops/runbooks/postgres-ha.md.

title: Scale Valkey to 3-Node Sentinel Configuration
subtasks: Reconfigure the Opstree Redis operator CR to deploy a 3-replica Valkey setup with Sentinel enabled (3 Sentinel instances monitoring the primary). Update the CR kind to RedisSentinel or equivalent operator-supported HA mode.
Configure maxmemory-policy on all Valkey nodes to allkeys-lru with maxmemory set to 80% of the pod memory limit.
Update the sigma1-infra-endpoints ConfigMap VALKEY_SIGMA1_URL to use the Sentinel-aware connection string (sentinel://<sentinel-svc>:26379/mymaster) or the operator-managed primary Service.
Verify automatic failover by deleting the primary Valkey pod and confirming Sentinel promotes a replica within 30 seconds. Run redis-cli PING against the primary Service before and after failover.
Update all service connection configurations to use Sentinel-aware Redis client settings (connection-manager reconnect on failover).

title: Configure TLS Certificates via cert-manager with Let's Encrypt
subtasks: Deploy cert-manager (if not already installed) using Helm chart jetstack/cert-manager with CRDs enabled. Verify cert-manager pods are Running in cert-manager namespace.
Create a ClusterIssuer CR for Let's Encrypt production using DNS-01 challenge solver with Cloudflare DNS API token (stored as Kubernetes Secret). Configure for *.sigma1.com wildcard certificate.
Create Certificate CRs for chat.sigma1.com, signals.sigma1.com, and api.sigma1.com. Verify certificates are issued and stored as TLS Secrets in the sigma1 namespace.
Update the cloudflared Tunnel configuration to use the cert-manager-issued TLS certificates for origin server verification (optional: Cloudflare Full Strict mode).
Create a PrometheusRule alert for certificate expiry within 14 days using cert-manager's built-in metrics (certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds).

title: Configure Cloudflare CDN for Static Assets and API Caching
subtasks: Configure Cloudflare CDN caching rules for the R2 bucket serving equipment images: Cache-Control max-age=86400 for image/* content types. Set up a custom domain (assets.sigma1.com) pointing to the R2 bucket via Cloudflare CDN.
Create Cloudflare Page Rules or Cache Rules for the website (sigma1.com): cache static assets (_next/static/*) with edge TTL of 30 days, bypass cache for /api/* routes.
Enable Cloudflare Polish (image optimization) and Brotli compression for the website domain.
Configure Cloudflare WAF rules: rate limiting (100 req/min per IP on /api/*), block known bad user agents, enable managed OWASP ruleset.
Verify CDN cache hit ratio by checking Cloudflare Analytics dashboard after a k6 load test hitting static asset URLs. Target > 90% cache hit rate for images.

title: Deploy Kubernetes Network Policies for Default-Deny and Namespace Isolation
subtasks: Create a default-deny CiliumClusterwideNetworkPolicy that blocks all ingress and egress traffic across the sigma1, databases, openclaw, and signal namespaces by default. This serves as the baseline policy on top of the per-service policies created in Task 10.
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy allowing DNS egress (port 53 UDP/TCP to kube-dns) for all pods in sigma1, databases, openclaw, and signal namespaces — required for service discovery.

Create CiliumNetworkPolicy allowing the databases namespace (postgres, valkey) to accept ingress only from pods in the sigma1 namespace with specific app labels (equipment-catalog, rms, finance, customer-vetting, social-engine, morgan).
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy for the signal namespace allowing ingress to signal-cli-svc only from the morgan pod in the openclaw namespace.

Validate all policies with a comprehensive connectivity matrix test: run a test pod in each namespace attempting HTTP connections to every service, documenting which connections are allowed vs blocked. Generate a connectivity report.

title: Deploy OpenTelemetry Collector with Tracing and Metrics Pipeline
subtasks: Deploy the OpenTelemetry Collector as a DaemonSet in the sigma1 namespace using the opentelemetry-collector Helm chart. Configure receivers: otlp (gRPC :4317, HTTP :4318), prometheus (scrape all service /metrics endpoints).

Configure the OTel Collector pipeline processors: batch (timeout: 5s, send_batch_size: 512), probabilistic_sampler (sampling.percentage: 10 for production, 100 for staging), memory_limiter (limit_mib: 512).

Configure exporters: send traces to Datadog APM using the Datadog exporter (already have Datadog agent in cluster). Send metrics to Datadog metrics endpoint. Configure the Datadog API key from existing datadog-agent secrets.

Instrument all backend services to export OTLP traces: add OpenTelemetry SDK initialization to equipment-catalog (Rust: opentelemetry-otlp crate), RMS (Go: go.opentelemetry.io/otel), finance (Rust), customer-vetting (Rust), and social-engine (Node.js: @opentelemetry/sdk-node). Document SDK setup per language.

Create a Datadog dashboard showing distributed trace visualization across services: Morgan → equipment-catalog → postgres, Morgan → RMS → postgres, etc. Verify end-to-end trace propagation by issuing a Morgan tool call and finding the full trace in Datadog.

title: Configure Prometheus Alert Rules for SLO Violations
subtasks: Create PrometheusRule CRs (or Datadog monitor equivalents) for latency SLOs: equipment-catalog p99 > 500ms for 5 minutes, RMS p99 > 1s for 5 minutes, finance p99 > 1s for 5 minutes. Route alerts to a dedicated Slack/Discord channel or Datadog monitor.
Create error rate alert rules: any service returning > 1% 5xx responses over a 2-minute window. Use the http_requests_total counter with status label filtering.
Create pod availability alert rules: any Deployment with desired replicas != available replicas for > 5 minutes. Include HPA-managed services.
Create database-specific alerts: CNPG replication lag > 1 second for 5 minutes, Valkey memory usage > 90% of maxmemory, PgBouncer connection pool exhaustion (active connections > 80% of max).
Create Morgan-specific alerts: chat response time > 10 seconds for 3 consecutive requests, Signal-CLI pod restart count > 2 in 10 minutes.
Create a Datadog or Grafana SLO dashboard aggregating all alert statuses with a 30-day SLO burn rate visualization for each service.
